name: Oatcakes (Scottish)
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Bacon Fat or Melted Butter
recipeInstructions:
1. To test the correct heat of the griddle, sprinkle it with a little flour. If the flour browns immediately, it is too hot; it should take a few seconds to turn color.
2. Set the oven to 375°F/Gas 5 or heat a griddle or heavy frying pan. Mix the oatmeal, bicarbonate of soda, and salt together in a bowl. Add the melted fat and hot water. Stir well until it forms a soft paste.
3. Sprinkle some oatmeal on a board. From the dough, form a round and roll it out as thinly as possible, adding oatmeal to the surface as needed to prevent sticking. Brush off excess oatmeal.
4. Cut the dough into 4 or 6 pieces. Place the oatcakes on a large ungreased baking sheet and bake in the oven for 15-20 minutes.
5. Alternatively, griddle bake the oatcakes until the edges begin to curl. Turn them over and cook the other side. Do not let the oatcakes brown; they should be a pale fawn color.
6. Place on a wire rack to cool. They are delicious served with cheese.
